summary: Flawless soundboard capture of a legendary 5/22/77 Florida show – pristine audio and a jaw‑dropping set highlighted by an epic Help > Slip > Franklin’s and the best‑ever Sugaree.
review: The recording is a crystal‑clear SBD that lets every nuance of Jerry’s soaring lead, Phil’s thunderous bass and Keith’s subtle riffs shine, while the audience ambience adds just enough live feel. Musically the band is on fire – opening with a tight “Music to Open,” delivering a sublime Sugaree, an unmatched Help > Slip > Franklin’s sequence and a seamless Wharf Rat → Terrapin → Morning Dew flow that many fans call one of ’77’s finest second‑sets.

ai_rating.rationale: Over thirty reviews unanimously praise both performance and sound quality; standout songs, flawless playing and a pristine soundboard dominate the feedback, yielding an exceptional combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, balanced mix with virtually no drop‑outs or muddiness; vocals and instruments sit perfectly together, delivering a near‑studio clarity that fans call the best soundboard of any Dead show.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
Estimated > Eyes > Wharf Rat > Terrapin > Dawn
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set rolls like a river – tight opening jams lead into a blistering first half, while the second set glides through seamless segues (Terrapin → Morning Dew, Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s) delivering continuous momentum and emotional peaks.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for his soaring tone, monster solos on Sugaree and the Help>Slip>Franklin's jam – called the best ever heard.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for “thundering” bass work, especially during the opening Music and throughout the second set.
band_member_comments.Bob: His rhythm riffs described as non‑duplicable and sweet, with special nods to his contributions on Peggy‑O and other cuts.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s simple repetitive riff in Help>Slip>Franklin's highlighted; teased motifs (later linked to Foolish Heart) noted by several reviewers.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey praised for driving the band, especially during Eyes of the World and the Wharf Rat → Terrapin transition.
